candidate: Adopt Week 19 VPS package as self-hosted baseline
why_now: It is the completed handoff-ready package
entry_condition: A team can own Docker, DNS, backup, updates, and incidents
first_action: Deploy to staging using compose.yaml and .env.template
success_metric: Teammate can explain architecture, startup, backup, risks, and next steps
avoid_until: Do not put business-critical workflows on it before restore drill

why_now: Credentials and workflow state must be recoverable
entry_condition: Any production workflow stores real credentials or business data
first_action: Run pg_dump and restore to staging
success_metric: Restore proves /healthz/readiness, credential decrypt, and webhook execution
avoid_until: Do not rely on backups that were never restored

entry_condition: Instance is reachable from the internet or uses sensitive credentials
first_action: Run n8n audit and record findings
success_metric: Findings have severity, owner, and due date
avoid_until: Do not expand public workflows before high-risk items are owned

candidate: Execution data retention and DB pruning
why_now: Database growth is the most likely silent cost and performance risk
entry_condition: DB size trend or execution count grows each week
first_action: Set retention and review saved success executions
success_metric: DB growth trend is stable and documented
avoid_until: Do not increase workflow volume with unlimited retention

why_now: Week 17 showed logs are required for fast diagnosis
entry_condition: Incidents cannot be diagnosed from local logs alone
first_action: Route n8n, Caddy, and PostgreSQL logs to a searchable place
success_metric: Incident note includes exact timestamp and process evidence
avoid_until: Do not add workers before logs are searchable

why_now: Usage-priced platforms and storage can drift
entry_condition: PaaS, Cloud Run, AWS, object storage, or multi-client delivery is used
success_metric: Monthly cost has owner and alert threshold
avoid_until: Do not accept open-ended autoscaling without budget

why_now: Some teams should not own infrastructure
first_action: Estimate executions and compare Cloud plan fit
success_metric: Decision records execution estimate and owner
avoid_until: Do not self-host only to avoid subscription cost

entry_condition: p95 latency, active executions, or webhook backlog breaches threshold twice
success_metric: Queue mode decision includes DB connection and rollback plan
avoid_until: Do not add Redis/workers before PostgreSQL, logs, and backup are stable

entry_condition: VPC, IAM, audit, SSO, RPO/RTO, and on-call are real requirements
success_metric: Security and finance approve or defer
avoid_until: Do not move to AWS/GCP for appearance or premature scale
